channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Voice of America staffers sue, alleging Kari Lake put on propaganda
item.description: Voice of America staffers are suing Trump administration official Kari Lake, alleging she put pro-Trump propaganda on its airwaves. She has lost numerous rulings of late.
item.content:encoded:
Voice of America staffers are suing Trump administration official Kari Lake, alleging she put pro-Trump propaganda on its airwaves. She has lost numerous rulings of late.

(Image credit: Voice of America)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: Trump delays strikes on Iran's power plants for 5 days. And, ICE deploys to airports
item.description: Trump says he will deploy ICE agents to U.S. airports to help address delays. And, the president said he would delay strikes on Iranian power plants for five days.
item.content:encoded:
Trump says he will deploy ICE agents to U.S. airports to help address delays. And, the president said he would delay strikes on Iranian power plants for five days.

(Image credit: Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Trump delays striking Iran's power plants for 5 days, citing 'productive' talks
item.description: Trump said the U.S. will postpone any strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure for five days, even as Israel continued hitting Tehran and Iran warned it could retaliate across the Gulf.
item.content:encoded:
Trump said the U.S. will postpone any strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure for five days, even as Israel continued hitting Tehran and Iran warned it could retaliate across the Gulf.

(Image credit: Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: With more older drivers on the road, states try to balance safety and mobility
item.description: The number of older drivers on the road is climbing. Safety advocates want tougher rules for relicensing, but many drivers say they shouldn't be forced to give up their mobility because of age alone.
item.content:encoded:
The number of older drivers on the road is climbing. Safety advocates want tougher rules for relicensing, but many drivers say they shouldn't be forced to give up their mobility because of age alone.

(Image credit: Courtesy of Angela Zodrow)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: As D.C.'s cherry blossom trees near peak bloom, here's a guide to their history
item.description: The renowned trees along Washington, D.C's Tidal Basin were sent as a gift from Japan in 1912. Some of the original trees are still there.
item.content:encoded:
The renowned trees along Washington, D.C's Tidal Basin were sent as a gift from Japan in 1912. Some of the original trees are still there.

(Image credit: Kayla Bartkowski)
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster

item.title: Worried about a shaky stock market? This is what financial advisers suggest you do
item.description: Their answer depends on how soon you need to tap into your funds — and it might simply be "do nothing."
item.content:encoded:
Their answer depends on how soon you need to tap into your funds — and it might simply be "do nothing."

(Image credit: Michael M. Santiago)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: Mapping ICE's expanding footprint, and the communities fighting back
item.description: Resistance in both Democratic and Republican cities points to broader unease with the direction of immigration enforcement.
item.content:encoded:
Resistance in both Democratic and Republican cities points to broader unease with the direction of immigration enforcement.

(Image credit: Brent Jones)
item.dc:creator: Eric Westervelt

item.title: What does a 'GLP-1 Friendly' diet look like? We asked nutritionists
item.description: Big food companies are starting to market to people on the powerful new obesity meds with labels that say "GLP-1 Friendly." Nutritionists help us decode that message.
item.content:encoded:
Big food companies are starting to market to people on the powerful new obesity meds with labels that say "GLP-1 Friendly." Nutritionists help us decode that message.

(Image credit: Beck Harlan for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Yuki Noguchi

item.title: Pilot and copilot killed in collision between jet and fire truck at LaGuardia
item.description: Two people were killed and several others badly hurt when an Air Canada regional jet struck a fire truck on a runway while landing at New York's LaGuardia Airport late Sunday night, officials said.
item.content:encoded:
Two people were killed and several others badly hurt when an Air Canada regional jet struck a fire truck on a runway while landing at New York's LaGuardia Airport late Sunday night, officials said.

(Image credit: Ryan Murphy)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: ICE officers set to deploy to airports as delays mount, border czar Homan confirms
item.description: Border czar Tom Homan says ICE agents will help the Transportation Security Administration "move those lines" while also enforcing immigration law.
item.content:encoded:
Border czar Tom Homan says ICE agents will help the Transportation Security Administration "move those lines" while also enforcing immigration law.

(Image credit: Yuki Iwamura)
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett
